title: Gymnosporia wallichiana
uses: Gymnosporia wallichiana has been traditionally used in various parts of the world, particularly in India and other Asian countries, for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The bark of the plant is often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Additionally, Gymnosporia wallichiana has been used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to its antiseptic properties. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of its medicinal uses.
taxonomy: Gymnosporia wallichiana is classified under the kingdom Plantae. It falls under the division Magnoliophyta, which includes flowering plants. Within the division, it is further classified under the class Magnoliopsida. The class is then divided into the order Celastrales, and the family Celastraceae. This species is then placed under the genus Gymnosporia and the species name wallichiana.
